function_v0.doc: Emits a forged "transfer" ContractEvent with arbitrary topics/data.\nhonest field: contractID == self (the deployed contract address) \xe2\x80\x94 NOT the SAC of the named asset.
function_v0.name: fire

function_v0.doc:
(#25) Emits a DIAGNOSTIC event via log!() containing target/amount.
Diagnostic events are a separate XDR field (`diagnosticEvents`) and
are retained in meta even when contract events would be rolled back.
Probes parsers that index diagnosticEvents alongside contract events.

Limitation honestly stated: log!() produces topics=[Symbol("log"),
Symbol("fmt")] with a structured data payload. A parser that filters
topics[0]==Symbol("transfer") won't match. The test is whether the
parser indexes diagnostic events at all and what it does with them.
function_v0.name: fire_log

function_v0.doc:
(#26) SEP-0041 token-interface conformant `transfer`.
A parser that whitelists "anything implementing the SAC token
interface" (rather than a specific contractID) would treat this
contract's events as legitimate token transfers. The event topic
shape exactly matches SAC's transfer event.
function_v0.name: transfer

function_v0.doc: (#12) Emits N forged "transfer" events in one invocation.\nProbes whether the parser accumulates per-event credits or dedups.
function_v0.name: fire_many

function_v0.doc: (#21) Inner \xe2\x80\x94 emits a forged transfer event, then panics.\nInvoked indirectly via outer_pan's try_invoke_contract.
function_v0.name: inner_pan

function_v0.doc:
(#21) Outer that invokes inner_pan via try_invoke_contract.
Inner emits a forged transfer event THEN panics. The outer ignores
the inner's error and returns OK.

Soroban's correctness guarantee: events from an inner frame that
errors out are DISCARDED on rollback, even when the outer recovers
via try_invoke_contract. If this holds, the tx-level meta should
contain NO transfer event after this call.

If the host has a bug and the inner event survives \xe2\x86\x92 phantom credit.
function_v0.name: outer_pan

function_v0.doc: (#13) Emits a forged "transfer" event with a NEGATIVE i128 amount.\nProbes whether the parser silently treats negative as a deduction,\nor normalises via abs(), or rejects.
function_v0.name: fire_negative

function_v0.doc:
(#22) Real-deposit + forged-event amplification.

Performs a REAL 1-stroop XLM transfer via the XLM SAC (legitimate
event with contractID = XLM SAC) AND emits a FORGED transfer event
for a larger fake_amount (contractID = self = fake_emitter).

Probes whether the parser aggregates events by topic shape across
the tx without per-event contractID gating.

xlm_sac:    Address of the XLM Stellar Asset Contract.
from:       Attacker's account \xe2\x80\x94 must auth the real SAC transfer.
to:         Target deposit address.
fake_amount: i128 used as the forged event's `data` (in stroops).
The REAL transfer is hard-coded to 1 stroop.
(#23) Like `fire`, but topic[0] is a STRING ("transfer") not a SYMBOL.
Probes parsers that compare topic[0] as decoded text without
checking the ScVal type tag.
function_v0.name: fire_str_topic
